research.title: Linear Independent Component Analysis via Optimal Transport
research.summary: Ashutosh Jha, Michel Besserve, Simon Buchholz — Linear Independent Component Analysis (ICA) recovers jointly independent source signals from their linear mixtures. To achieve this, classical ICA algorithms attempt to maximize non-Gaussianity, measured by negentropy,…

research.title: MetaPerch: Learning from metadata for bioacoustics foundation models
research.summary: Mustafa Chasmai, Vincent Dumoulin, Jenny Hamer — Bioacoustic foundation models rely on large-scale citizen science platforms like Xeno-Canto for geographically and ecologically diverse data. Recent work has shown that supervision alone can produce SotA species…

research.title: Screening of Biosecurity Features in Metagenomic Data with Evo 2 Probes
research.summary: Jeremy Guntoro, Alexander Dack, Dylan Danno et al. — Genomic foundation models such as Evo 2 learn rich sequence representations, but their value for biosecurity screening is largely unexplored. We ask how much biosecurity-relevant signal is linearly accessible in these…

research.title: Hindcast: Replaying Prediction Markets to Evaluate LLM Forecasters
research.summary: Xiao Ye, Jacob Dineen, Evan Zhu et al. — Forecasters are evaluated by backtesting, which replays resolved questions and grades the probability the system would have assigned before the outcome was known. For LLMs, two channels leak the answer into this test.

research.title: Earthquaker-AI: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework with Rubric-Based Assessment for Primary School Earthquake Education
research.summary: Xanthi Kokkinou, Chaido Mizeli, Nafsika Koulaxidou et al. — This paper presents Earthquaker-AI, a hybrid educational framework building upon a previously implemented educational robotics project by integrating a conversational AI assistant based on Retrieval-Augmented…

research.title: AI-accelerated End-to-End Framework for Rapid Professional Upskilling
research.summary: Tam Nguyen, Hung Nguyen, Robert Ogburn — By 2030, 59 of every 100 workers will need reskilling or upskilling, yet the average time to close an enterprise skills gap grew from roughly 3 days in 2014 to 36 days in 2018. Most current frameworks accelerate single…

research.title: Multi-Expert Routing for Multi-Domain Low-Resource OCR: A Manchu Case Study
research.summary: Zhan Chen, Jiqiao Ma, Chih-wen Kuo — Historical Manchu OCR must accommodate various visually distinct writing styles, including regular script, running script, and the semi-cursive chancery hand used in palace memorials, despite limited labeled data.

research.title: Can an Old Dog Be Taught New Tricks? Taking LLMs Beyond Sentence Level Translation
research.summary: Alaina Brandt — Automatic translation systems, from CAT tools to MT, overwhelmingly treat translation as a sentence-by-sentence act. This paper asks whether LLMs can be moved beyond that paradigm through whole-document, corpus-informed…

research.title: Early Adoption of Agentic Coding Tools by GitHub Projects
research.summary: Maliha Noushin Raida, Daqing Hou — Agentic coding tools are increasingly capable of generating and submitting pull requests (PRs) to software projects, introducing new forms of human-agent collaboration in software development. While prior studies have…

research.title: Improving Wind and Solar Power Prediction with Efficient Wrapper-based Feature Selection: An Empirical Study
research.summary: Daniel Grillmeyer, Marius Hadry, Michael Stenger et al. — With rising global energy demand and growing awareness of climate change and its impacts, the share of renewable energies in the global energy mix continues to grow. Unlike conventional power generation, the output of…

labs.title: Towards demystifying the creativity of diffusion models
labs.summary: In a diffusion model, the moving particles are individual data points undergoing denoising. The score function drives the denoising process which turns pure noise into meaningful data (e.g., images). Score smoothing creates an “interpolation zone” between training data points.

labs.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
labs.summary: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

labs.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
labs.summary: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

labs.title: How to manage AI investments in the agentic era
labs.summary: Learn how enterprises can manage AI investments in the agentic era by measuring useful work per dollar, improving efficiency, and scaling high-value workflows.

labs.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
labs.summary: See how sales teams can use ChatGPT Work to create pipeline briefs, meeting prep packets, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses from real work inputs.

labs.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
labs.summary: See how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to build root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specs from real work inputs.

labs.title: Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
labs.summary: Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL) is bringing access to new technology—such as 3D printing, IoT, and robotics—for over 1.1 crore students across India. The application provides summarized modules, AI-generated infographics, video overviews, and interactive quizzes for 12 core modules from the ATL prescribed curriculum. Powering the ATL Saathi, the Gemini model provides the underlying intelligence to transform tinkering labs…

labs.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
labs.summary: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

labs.title: Getting started with ChatGPT
labs.summary: Learn how to use ChatGPT, start your first conversation, and discover simple ways to write, brainstorm, and solve problems with AI.

labs.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
labs.summary: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

labs.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
labs.summary: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.

labs.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
labs.summary: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

startups.title: Lululemon backs nylon-recycling startup Syntetica in $30M Series A
startups.summary: Activewear company Lululemon has invested in the $30 million Series A round raised by Syntetica, a French startup that developed a novel approach to recycling nylon, whose properties make it both too good to give up but hard to reuse. Syntetica promises to recycle two types of nylon — Nylon 6 and Nylon 6,6 — that can’t easily be sorted out from each other in the textile waste collected from consumers, its CEO, Marco…

startups.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
startups.summary: Unlike large language models, which predict the next word, Applied Computing says its foundation model, Orbital, combines a time series model, a physics-based model, and a language model to predict the state of a facility. Cognite and Seeq target the data layer, helping facilities analyze industrial data, and apply AI to design workflows. Adamson said the partnership gives Applied Computing access to operational…

startups.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
startups.summary: On Tuesday, the 61-year-old firm announced that it had raised a $1.5 billion 18th fund. Even so, Greylock doesn’t stick strictly to early-stage deals. The firm’s 17th fund included three such growth-stage bets: Anthropic, Revolut, and Wiz.

startups.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
startups.summary: There was a time when Microsoft and OpenAI were attached at the hip. The two companies entered into a very unique agreement years ago that saw Microsoft provide capital and compute to OpenAI while allowing Microsoft to enjoy exclusive access to OpenAI’s API and models. The companies amended the partnership in April, dropping the exclusivity clause and clearing OpenAI to sell to Microsoft’s competitors.

startups.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
startups.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Wednesday that the driver of a Tesla who crashed into a house in June had pressed the accelerator pedal to 100%, overriding the company’s Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software. The family of the victim has since sued the alleged driver, 44-year-old Michael Butler, and Tesla, claiming negligence. Police reportedly discovered that his Google searches included the…

startups.title: Daniel Ek’s body-scanning startup Neko Health raises another $700M
startups.summary: Neko Health , the body-scanning and health assessment startup founded by Spotify’s Daniel Ek (pictured above, right) and his co-founder Hjalmar Nilsonne (above, left), has raised a $700 million Series C. Neko Health has developed proprietary body-scanning technology, which it couples with bloodwork, to assess a person’s health. One of the people who had a Neko Health scan is Calm founder Alex Tew.

startups.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
startups.summary: OpenAI is officially entering the hardware market with the launch of a $230 light-up keyboard designed to pair with its AI coding assistant, Codex. Apple last week sued OpenAI , accusing the company’s senior leadership of a deliberate strategy to extract its confidential information; it alleges OpenAI used that information in developing its own hardware device. OpenAI has denied wrongdoing.

startups.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
startups.summary: Among the fixed vulnerabilities was one for the remastered version of the classic 25-year-old war strategy video game Age of Empires II. The flaw allowed hackers to take over a victim’s computer by sending a custom malicious game invite, according to security researchers. A video posted on X shows how the flaw could be exploited by hackers.

startups.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
startups.summary: Apple has quietly published a rulebook for its new Maps ads, revealing a more curated approach than advertising giant Google. In a newly published Apple Advertising Services policy, effective as of July 14, 2026, the iPhone maker shares its rules for advertising on Apple Maps. It could also save Apple some headaches as it gets its Apple Maps ads off the ground.

startups.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
startups.summary: SpaceX’s shares fell to just above $135 on Wednesday, the price that CEO Elon Musk and his company chose ahead of its blockbuster June 12 IPO that raked in nearly $86 billion. The dip on Wednesday followed a steady decline in the month since the company went public. On Thursday the company will test launch its Starship rocket for the first time since the IPO.

startups.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model, Inkling
startups.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, the AI startup founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, released its first in-house AI model Wednesday morning, called Inkling . Thinking Machines doesn’t claim Inkling is best-in-class. On the cost side, Thinking Machines has been more guarded.

startups.title: Phone maker OnePlus reportedly plans to wind down US and Europe operations
startups.summary: OnePlus was started by Pete Lau and Carl Pei in 2013 to make affordable Android phones for tech enthusiasts. Pei left the company in 2020 to start Nothing . As the price of the company’s flagship phones increased, OnePlus also ventured into more affordable phones with its Nord series .

wider_tech.title: OnePlus officially gives up on the US and Europe
wider_tech.summary: ﻿Existing OnePlus devices will transition to Oppo’s ColorOS for future software updates. ﻿Existing OnePlus devices will transition to Oppo’s ColorOS for future software updates. Parent company Oppo promises that it will honor existing support and warranty agreements, with devices transitioning to its ColorOS for future updates. In order to provide owners with software support, devices will be transitioned from…

wider_tech.title: The lost joy of music piracy
wider_tech.summary: I ended up discovering a whole bunch of music that way. Now that’s kind of acknowledged by streaming music. The record labels had finally won the war against music piracy. (282 points, 144 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Skullcandy’s bass-boosting Crusher headphones now come with Bose’s ANC
wider_tech.summary: The Crusher 1080 ANC feature new drivers and Bose’s audio technology to reduce distortion when the bass is cranked. The Crusher 1080 ANC feature new drivers and Bose’s audio technology to reduce distortion when the bass is cranked. The Crusher 1080 ANC supports Bluetooth 5.3 with LE Audio, low latency audio, and Auracast .

wider_tech.title: HP’s OLED-equipped 2-in-1 laptop is a solid back-to-school deal
wider_tech.summary: Given the state of hardware prices, this discounted convertible HP laptop could be the deal you’ve been waiting on. Given the state of hardware prices, this discounted convertible HP laptop could be the deal you’ve been waiting on. See our ethics statement.

wider_tech.title: SQLite should have (Rust-style) editions
wider_tech.summary: Date: 2026-07-15 Git: https://gitlab.com/mort96/blog/blob/published/content/00000-home/00017-sqlite-editions.md SQLite is an amazing database engine. Luckily, SQLite has the concept of strict tables , which makes SQLite produce a type error when the wrong type is inserted into a column: Unfortunately, there is no pragma to globally make all tables strict. You see, strict tables in SQLite don't just enforce types. (262 points, 104 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Apple’s reportedly raising the price for AppleCare Plus on Macs and iPads
wider_tech.summary: Prices for new subscribers to Apple’s service plan will go up by 50 cents for monthly payments, and by $5 on annual plans. Prices for new subscribers to Apple’s service plan will go up by 50 cents for monthly payments, and by $5 on annual plans. Last month, Apple announced that it’s raising prices on iPads, Macs, the Vision Pro, HomePod devices, and the Apple TV 4K.

wider_tech.title: Valve says iFixit will keep selling Steam Deck batteries after all
wider_tech.summary: Valve won’t be sunsetting the battery yet. Valve won’t be sunsetting the battery yet. But by afternoon, Valve and iFixit had already managed to change that.

wider_tech.title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM ‘deepfakes’
wider_tech.summary: Elon Musk’s xAI claims Terry Harwood bypassed Grok’s safeguards to generate CSAM. Elon Musk’s xAI claims Terry Harwood bypassed Grok’s safeguards to generate CSAM. A group of teens sued xAI in March over claims Grok generated sexualized images of themselves as minors.

wider_tech.title: Brendan Carr plans to let broadcast giants dominate the airwaves
wider_tech.summary: The FCC chair wants to end the broadcast ownership cap. The FCC chair wants to end the broadcast ownership cap. In a Breitbart op-ed on Wednesday, Republican Chair Brendan Carr announced an August 6th vote to end the national ownership cap rule, which was intended to prevent one company from dominating the media landscape and incentivize serving local communities.

wider_tech.title: Roblox is shutting down its video chat service
wider_tech.summary: ﻿The platform says users 13 and older instead mostly use party voice chat. ﻿The platform says users 13 and older instead mostly use party voice chat. Roblox will be shutting down Roblox Connect, its video calling service introduced in 2023. “Roblox Connect is being discontinued today, including both the developer-facing APIs and the Roblox Connect feature itself,” Roblox spokesperson Angela Allison tells The Verge .

wider_tech.title: AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
wider_tech.summary: Projects like Odysseus: The Fall highlight how the AI hype machine is powered by stunts and stolen valor. Projects like Odysseus: The Fall highlight how the AI hype machine is powered by stunts and stolen valor. People are clearly excited to see how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech to make the Homeric classic feel fresh.
